title: That Darn Katz

jokes.text: Cash, cash, cash for your bones. Too many bones, not enough cash? Call Cashbone. Ribs, skulls, spines, even certain tiny ear bones. The leg bone's connected to the Cashbone.
jokes.setup: Parody of cash-for-gold commercials
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific bone types culminating in riff on 'Dem Bones' song
jokes.explanation: Strong wordplay and absurd specificity ('certain tiny ear bones') with good musical payoff. Classic Futurama commercial parody.

jokes.text: Aw, Nibbler made a bobo for Mama. I'll pick it up with my supey-dupey poopy scoopy.
jokes.setup: Pet owner baby talk
jokes.punchline: Absurdly infantilizing language for cleaning up excrement
jokes.explanation: Character-specific cringe humor showing Leela's over-attachment to Nibbler through ridiculous baby talk.

jokes.text: Whoa! The magma P.I. is 10,000 degrees Selleck.
jokes.setup: Scientific temperature reading
jokes.punchline: Tom Selleck's name as temperature unit
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'Magnum P.I.' with unexpected celebrity name as unit of measurement. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: You've been my grad student for 12 years. You were ready six years ago. What?! I probably should have told you.
jokes.setup: Amy's nervousness about her thesis defense
jokes.punchline: Professor casually admits to withholding her readiness for years
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for absent-minded professor. The casual delivery of devastating news is well-structured.

jokes.text: Do what you young people do to unwind. Take a joyride in your jalopy. Wear a T-shirt and eat a fish stick.
jokes.setup: Professor giving modern advice to Amy
jokes.punchline: Hilariously outdated and specific 'youth activities'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Farnsworth's disconnect from modern youth through absurdly specific outdated activities.

jokes.text: Still a little nervous? (burps) She's a nervous wreck!
jokes.setup: Amy supposedly drinking to relax
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude observation paired with Amy's obvious intoxication
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy with Bender's characteristic bluntness. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Leela, it's time you and I had a talk. A talk?! You can't break up with me. You're my pet.
jokes.setup: Nibbler wanting to have 'a talk' with Leela
jokes.punchline: Leela immediately assumes pet breakup scenario
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of relationship dynamics with Leela's panicked response to pet 'breakup.' Good character integration.

jokes.text: Aw, is shnookums not feeling 'shtimuwated'?
jokes.setup: Nibbler complaining about lack of intellectual stimulation
jokes.punchline: Leela's condescending baby talk version of 'stimulated'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's inability to take Nibbler seriously, with cute wordplay twist.

jokes.text: I keep telling you, that's my real naval uniform.
jokes.setup: Leela mentioning dressing Nibbler in cute sailor suit
jokes.punchline: Nibbler's dignified correction about his actual military status
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Nibbler's secret identity with perfect economy. Character-specific humor that rewards series knowledge.

jokes.text: Your place or mine? Both. But first, this place. Room for one more?
jokes.setup: Romantic exchange between Fry and Amy
jokes.punchline: Bender's inappropriate interruption of intimate moment
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate humor for Bender but relies on familiar sexual comedy tropes. Functional but not inspired.

jokes.text: Sorry... I hooked up with Bender last night. Dude was all over my snooze button.
jokes.setup: Amy's alarm clock being late
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphized alarm clock describing sexual encounter with robot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor with perfect double entendre on 'snooze button.' Original concept with excellent execution.

jokes.text: Kif, did you yack on the floor? Yes, I did.
jokes.setup: Someone has vomited on the floor
jokes.punchline: Kif's matter-of-fact admission without shame or explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Kif's resigned acceptance of humiliation. Maximum economy with deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: I see by your curriculum vitae that you're a Sagittarius.
jokes.setup: Academic examining Amy's qualifications
jokes.punchline: Professor focuses on astrological sign instead of academic credentials
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing academic pretension taken to ridiculous extreme. Clean setup and payoff.

jokes.text: The cow says... (cow moos) He proved that 50 years ago, and he's been coasting on it ever since.
jokes.setup: Introduction to examination committee member
jokes.punchline: Professor whose only achievement is proving what sound a cow makes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of academic stagnation. The mundane 'discovery' contrasted with 50 years of coasting is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Well, yes, but that's not 'revelant.'
jokes.setup: Amy asked if she's been drinking before her thesis defense
jokes.punchline: Drunk Amy mispronounces 'relevant' while claiming it's irrelevant
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Amy's intoxication through mispronunciation while making logical argument.

jokes.text: To many young men in the inner city, it is.
jokes.setup: Amy saying 'suppose this basketball is the whole world'
jokes.punchline: Professor's unexpectedly profound social commentary
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection from scientific metaphor to social commentary. Perfect economy and unexpected depth from minor character.

jokes.text: Yes, tapping the earth's ener-kerchoo.
jokes.setup: Amy trying to present scientific thesis while allergic to cats
jokes.punchline: Sneeze perfectly timed to complete word 'energy'
jokes.explanation: Well-timed physical comedy with wordplay element. The sneeze completing the word is nicely structured.

jokes.text: Professor Katz, would you mind...? Miss Wong, I mind everything.
jokes.setup: Amy asking professor to accommodate her cat allergy
jokes.punchline: Katz's blanket refusal to be considerate about anything
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment with economy - reveals Katz as completely unreasonable

jokes.text: The horse says, 'Doctorate denied.'
jokes.setup: Committee voting on Amy's thesis
jokes.punchline: Cow sound professor now doing horse sounds for academic judgment
jokes.explanation: Good callback that expands the character's gimmick logically. The formal phrasing makes the absurdity funnier.

jokes.text: I don't have a fancy degree either, but today I'm a prominent boy in the package delivery field.
jokes.setup: Fry consoling Amy about her failed thesis defense
jokes.punchline: Fry's misplaced pride in menial job using oddly formal language
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Fry's misguided optimism and status confusion. 'Prominent boy' is particularly funny phrasing.

jokes.text: Hey, look, a kitty cat.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth dismissing cute interruptions to focus on business
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandons authority when he sees the cat
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal showing even the crankiest character succumbing to cat cuteness. Clean setup and payoff.

jokes.text: Aw, its anus looks like an asterisk.
jokes.setup: Everyone cooing over the cute cat
jokes.punchline: Hermes makes uncomfortably specific anatomical observation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe humor that's both disgusting and oddly accurate. The clinical observation amid cuteness overload is perfect.

jokes.text: Captain Fuzzy Toes reporting for duty... I mean, cutie.
jokes.setup: Leela dressing up the cat in uniform
jokes.punchline: Self-correction from military to cutesy language
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate humor showing Leela caught between her military background and cat obsession.

jokes.text: My best friend died in that uniform.
jokes.setup: Everyone treating military uniform as cute costume
jokes.punchline: Nibbler's dark reality check about military service
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor that perfectly contrasts cute moment with war reality. Character-specific and emotionally resonant.

jokes.text: If I had a spray bottle, I'd give him such a spritzing.
jokes.setup: Nibbler expressing hatred for the space cat
jokes.punchline: Threatening language using ineffective cat discipline method
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Nibbler's dignity constrained by his pet appearance. The menacing tone with mild action is funny.

jokes.text: I'll tell you one thing... nothing acts that cute without some ulterior motive.
jokes.setup: Everyone being charmed by cute cat
jokes.punchline: Nibbler (himself a cute pet with hidden agenda) being suspicious
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Nibbler describing himself while accusing another

jokes.text: Here's my new theory: That is one adorable cat.
jokes.setup: Nibbler previously suspicious of the cat
jokes.punchline: Complete 180 after being exposed to cat's cuteness
jokes.explanation: Shows power of cat mind control - even suspicious Nibbler succumbs

jokes.text: Amy and Nibbler are a 'tewwible thweat.' They must be spayed and neutered, 'wespectivewy.'
jokes.setup: Evil cat plotting against heroes
jokes.punchline: Sinister plan delivered in cutesy baby talk voice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of evil plotting with cute speech impediment. The specific pet threats are darkly funny.

jokes.text: Meow-meow, meow-meow (increasingly distorted and deep)
jokes.setup: Cat singing innocent Meow Mix jingle
jokes.punchline: Song becomes ominous and demonic as it progresses
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation of familiar commercial jingle into something sinister. Good audio comedy that builds tension.

jokes.text: Of course it wanted a saucer, you idiot... a saucer of 'dewicious cweam.'
jokes.setup: Amy and Nibbler claiming cat summoned UFO saucer
jokes.punchline: Bender's logical explanation delivered in baby talk
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's dismissive logic corrupted by cat influence. The baby talk contradiction is effective.

jokes.text: It's pwonounced 'cweam.'
jokes.setup: Characters under cat influence arguing
jokes.punchline: Leela correcting pronunciation while using same baby talk
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of the baby talk corruption, showing how far the influence has spread through pedantic correction.

jokes.text: Where do you think you're going, No-cat? Uh, the basement. I think I left the air-hockey table on.
jokes.setup: Amy stopped by cat-influenced Bender
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous excuse about leaving air hockey table 'on'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor in the mundane excuse for a table that can't really be 'left on.' Amy's panic creates funny desperation.

jokes.text: We're certainly not building something sinister, if that's what you're implying. Now, come on, Bender, something sinister won't build itself.
jokes.setup: Bender denying sinister activity
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by mentioning sinister construction
jokes.explanation: Excellent immediate contradiction revealing the truth through Bender's characteristic obliviousness. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: You should have called my cousin, Turner.
jokes.setup: Bender unable to turn the device
jokes.punchline: References cousin named Turner for turning-related tasks
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that's character-appropriate for Bender's tendency to have specialized robot relatives. Clean and economical.

jokes.text: I warn you, if I have to get cute, it's gonna get ugly.
jokes.setup: Threatening Professor Katz
jokes.punchline: Paradoxical threat combining cuteness and violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay threat that captures Nibbler's dual nature perfectly - cute but dangerous

jokes.text: He's one of those dog-operated puppets that's been adapted for use by a cat!
jokes.setup: Discovering Professor Katz isn't dead
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific explanation for puppet professor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept that creates its own logic. The specificity of 'adapted for use by a cat' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: So I had my doctorate denied by that fuzz ball?!
jokes.setup: Discovering cat was controlling professor during thesis defense
jokes.punchline: Amy's outrage at being judged by a cat
jokes.explanation: Good callback to thesis failure with justified outrage at the absurd situation

jokes.text: Well, look what the me dragged in.
jokes.setup: Cat confronting captured heroes
jokes.punchline: Twist on 'look what the cat dragged in' with literal cat speaker
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works perfectly for the character. The self-referential twist on the idiom is well-executed.

jokes.text: You can talk! You can talk? How ironic! Two hyper-intelligent beings both pretending to be simple house pets.
jokes.setup: Mutual surprise at each other talking
jokes.punchline: Recognition of their parallel deceptions
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing both are more than they seem

jokes.text: Say, do you know Obliteron? He pretends to be a hamster, but... Yeah, that's really great. Could you please tell us what's going on?!
jokes.setup: Nibbler making small talk with villain
jokes.punchline: Amy's exasperated interruption of inappropriate timing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Nibbler's social awkwardness and Amy's desperation. The interruption timing works well.

jokes.text: Although we call it Blue-Blue Shiny Ball.
jokes.setup: Alien cats referring to Earth
jokes.punchline: Childlike naming convention for the planet
jokes.explanation: Effective alien perspective humor showing how cats would naturally name Earth. Simple but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Welcome to academia.
jokes.setup: Amy complaining about thesis being stolen
jokes.punchline: Cat's cynical summary of academic theft
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect economy. The casual acceptance of academic corruption is both funny and pointed.

jokes.text: Earth will be one big McDLT.
jokes.setup: Explaining Earth will be hot on one side, cold on the other
jokes.punchline: Reference to obsolete McDonald's burger with hot/cold concept
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture reference that perfectly matches the scientific concept. Nostalgic and clever analogy.

jokes.text: We just got that commercial last year.
jokes.setup: McDLT reference from the 1980s
jokes.punchline: Fry's confusion about timeline of obsolete commercial
jokes.explanation: Good character callback showing Fry's temporal confusion. Works as both character moment and meta commentary.

jokes.text: If you're propositioning me, I'm not interested.
jokes.setup: Cat asking 'Ever had a cat rub up against you?'
jokes.punchline: Amy's sexual misinterpretation of scientific question
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection humor but relies on fairly predictable sexual misunderstanding. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Soon the world will come to a stop, and there's nothing you can do to stop it. The stopping of the world, that is. It's unstoppable.
jokes.setup: Villain delivering dramatic threat
jokes.punchline: Over-explanation and redundant clarification ruins dramatic effect
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing villain's need to over-explain. The redundancy deflates the dramatic moment effectively.

jokes.text: The Earth h h is coming to a st!
jokes.setup: Amy trying to warn everyone while Earth is stopping
jokes.punchline: Her speech cuts off as she physically experiences the stopping
jokes.explanation: Effective physical comedy where the reality interrupts the exposition. Good integration of action and dialogue.

jokes.text: Catastrophe Beach Party... Scene One. Cue disaster effects and... action! Cut! That extra looked at the camera. We'll have to do it again.
jokes.setup: Apocalyptic scene during world ending
jokes.punchline: Revealed to be film production worried about continuity during actual catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta humor about film production continuing during actual apocalypse. The mundane concern about extras is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Catastrophe Beach Party... Scene One. Cue disaster effects and... action! Cut! That extra looked at the camera. We'll have to do it again.
jokes.setup: Global catastrophe unfolding
jokes.punchline: It's being filmed as a movie with typical film problems
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about disaster movies with perfectly timed film production complaints

jokes.text: What's all this cat hair on my sweater? Why am I full of sand? And Tootsie Rolls?
jokes.setup: Hermes waking up from cat hypnosis
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and disturbing evidence of lost time
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal to increasingly bizarre evidence. The Tootsie Rolls detail is particularly unsettling and funny.

jokes.text: Enjoy being broiled alive by the sun, and also the dead bird we've left in your slipper.
jokes.setup: Evil cat delivering final threat
jokes.punchline: Mundane cat behavior alongside apocalyptic threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent character humor combining cosmic villainy with typical annoying cat behavior. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You should have called my cousin, Turner.
jokes.setup: Trying to turn the ratchet backwards
jokes.punchline: Repeats earlier joke about specialized cousin
jokes.explanation: Weak callback that doesn't add anything new to the earlier joke. Feels repetitive rather than building on the concept.

jokes.text: You can't suddenly know something just by assembling a committee of words.
jokes.setup: Amy claiming sudden inspiration
jokes.punchline: Professor's dismissive description of brainstorming
jokes.explanation: Brilliant description of inspiration that's both cynical and accurate. 'Committee of words' is particularly clever phrasing.

jokes.text: You can't suddenly know something just by assembling a committee of words.
jokes.setup: Amy claiming to have sudden inspiration
jokes.punchline: Professor's cynical view of scientific breakthroughs
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on how scientific breakthroughs work in fiction

jokes.text: Yes... the wrong direction! So?
jokes.setup: Earth will start spinning backwards
jokes.punchline: Amy's casual dismissal of massive planetary change
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Amy's casual attitude toward massive consequences. The deadpan 'So?' is excellently timed.

jokes.text: On the whole, Earth society is worthless. But they do have these things called antique rugs that are great for peeing on.
jokes.setup: Cat reflecting on Earth civilization
jokes.punchline: Dismisses all human achievement except rug-peeing opportunities
jokes.explanation: Excellent character humor showing authentic cat priorities. The juxtaposition of cosmic dismissal with mundane cat behavior is perfect.

jokes.text: And though the world is now spinning in the wrong direction, it's good enough.
jokes.setup: Resolution of world-ending crisis
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of permanent planetary change as 'good enough'
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan resolution showing typical Futurama attitude toward massive changes. 'Good enough' perfectly captures the show's tone.

jokes.text: How's the job market? Ruff.
jokes.setup: Amy asking about job prospects with new PhD
jokes.punchline: Professor's dog pun expressing poor employment outlook
jokes.explanation: Solid dark humor about academic job market with simple but effective pun. Good reality check after celebration.

tmdb_overview: Earth is invaded by a race of intelligent cats.
